Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; Clementine
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata Clementine genome assembly/scaffold-based reference; exact version NA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavone O-methylation branch of citrus-specific PMF pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flavedo; also leaves and flowers noted for PMFs in citrus
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive citrus specialized metabolites associated with anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular-protective properties; plant barrier against pathogens and UV
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and converts intermediate PMFs/hydroxyflavones toward fully O-methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PMF accumulation in citrus flavedo; overexpression increases total PMFs and specific PMFs, while RNAi reduces them
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT3 was identified by BSA and transcriptome-metabolome integration as one of the key OMT genes associated with PMF accumulation. 2. Its expression was strongly correlated with total PMFs and six PMF monomers. 3. Recombinant and yeast assays showed multisite O-methylation activity, especially efficient methylation at C6 and C4' positions and activity on C3' and C7 for specific substrates. 4. In combination with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5, it enables full O-methylation of hydroxyflavones to generate diverse PMFs. 5. Overexpression in citrus callus and fruit flavedo increased PMFs, whereas RNAi suppression decreased PMFs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; transcriptome-metabolome correlation; cloning; heterologous expression in yeast; in vitro recombinant enzyme assays and kinetics; citrus callus overexpression; transient overexpression in flavedo; RNAi suppression; metabolite profiling by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosylmethionine to hydroxyl groups on hydroxyflavones, especially catalyzing C6 and C4' O-methylation with additional substrate-dependent C3' and C7 methylation, thereby producing polymethoxylated flavones
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones such as 5,6-OH-4',7-dimethoxyflavone (DMF1); 3'-demethylnobiletin; genkwanin; cirsimaritin; lysionotin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: O-methylated hydroxyflavones and PMF intermediates/products including nobiletin, sinensetin, 5,7,4'-trimethyl apigenin, TMF2, 8-OH-HXMF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in PMF-rich fruit flavedo of Red tangerine; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins and mandarin-derived cultivars; correlated with PMF accumulation during fruit development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue type (flavedo vs juice sac); developmental stage; transient overexpression/RNAi assays in flavedo
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key terminal modifying enzyme; major control point for natural variation in PMFs, but strict rate-limiting status not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter; no specific promoter engineering for CreOMT3 reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RNA-seq (BioProjects PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912); BSA/pool sequencing (BioProject PRJNA938971)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful target for PMF biofortification and breeding citrus with enhanced health-benefit metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of tandem OMT cluster likely duplicated from OMT6. Ortholog could not be cloned from trifoliata orange, pummelo, or Chinese box orange. Works complementarily with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5 rather than alone producing all PMFs.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Clementine; Mangshan mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata Clementine genome assembly/scaffold-based reference; exact version NA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavone O-methylation branch of citrus-specific PMF pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Polymethoxyflavones including nobiletin, tangeretin, sinensetin and related PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive PMFs with anticancer, anti-inflammatory and health-promoting activities; protective roles against pathogens and UV in plants
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and converts PMF intermediates such as 5DNOB and hydroxyflavones toward major PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong positive regulator/enzyme for PMF accumulation; overexpression elevates total PMFs and major PMFs, while RNAi lowers PMFs; promoter deletion reduces expression and PMF content in cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT4 was identified as a core PMF-biosynthetic gene by BSA and transcriptome-metabolome analyses. 2. It preferentially O-methylates C7 and C5 sites and can also catalyze C3, C6 and C3' methylation depending on substrate. 3. It efficiently converts intermediates such as 5DNOB toward nobiletin and collaborates with CreOMT3/5 to generate fully O-methylated PMFs. 4. Overexpression in callus and fruit flavedo increased multiple PMFs, whereas RNAi suppression reduced total PMFs and several major PMFs. 5. A 1,041-bp promoter fragment containing two MITEs boosts promoter activity and is associated with high PMF accumulation in wild/early-admixture mandarins. 6. N17 is essential for multisite O-methylation activity and helps explain functional divergence from OMT6.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; metabolome-transcriptome correlation; recombinant protein assays; kinetic analysis; citrus callus overexpression; transient overexpression in fruit flavedo; RNAi suppression; dual-luciferase promoter assay; site-directed mutagenesis; molecular docking
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to hydroxyflavones at multiple positions, especially C7 and C5, and also C3, C6, and C3' depending on substrate, enabling formation of multiple citrus PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including DMF1, 3'-demethylnobiletin, 3-OH-flavone, lysionotin, gardenin B, genkwanin, cirsimaritin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: O-methylated PMF intermediates and final PMFs including nobiletin, sinensetin, SAL, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in PMF-rich flavedo; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins; promoter fragment presence elevates expression; positively correlated with total PMFs during fruit development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue specificity; developmental stage; transient expression in citrus flavedo; promoter assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal modifying/control enzyme strongly supported; major contributor to natural variation in PMF content
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native OMT4 promoters from Clementine (short, 738 bp in assay context) and Mangshan mandarin (long, 1,779 bp) tested; 1,041-bp MITE-containing fragment enhances transcription
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RNA-seq (PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912); BSA/pool sequencing (PRJNA938971)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High-value target and molecular marker source for breeding citrus with elevated PMFs; 1,041-bp promoter fragment can assist selection of high-PMF germplasm
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CreOMT4-N17D loses most multisite activity except residual C7 methylation of LYS; reciprocal CreOMT6-D17N gains C3/C5/C6/C3' activity. Modern cultivated mandarins often lost the 1,041-bp promoter fragment, reducing expression and PMF accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Clementine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata Clementine genome assembly/scaffold-based reference; exact version NA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavone O-methylation branch of citrus-specific PMF pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Polymethoxyflavones including nobiletin, tangeretin, sinensetin and related PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive PMFs with human health benefits and protective roles in plants
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and converts intermediate hydroxyflavones/PMFs toward major PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PMF accumulation; overexpression increases total PMFs and major PMFs, while RNAi suppression lowers PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT5 was identified as one of the three core OMT cluster genes controlling PMF biosynthesis. 2. It preferentially O-methylates C7, C5 and C3' sites with strong catalytic activity on several hydroxyflavones. 3. It efficiently converts 3'DNOB and other intermediates toward highly methylated PMFs including nobiletin. 4. Overexpression in callus and flavedo increased PMF content, whereas RNAi reduced total PMFs and major components. 5. Together with CreOMT3 and CreOMT4, it complements regioselectivity gaps and enables full O-methylation to form diverse PMFs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; transcript-metabolite correlation; cloning; yeast expression assay; recombinant enzyme kinetics; citrus callus overexpression; transient overexpression in flavedo; RNAi suppression; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to hydroxyflavones, especially at C7, C5 and C3' positions, contributing to synthesis of highly O-methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3'-demethylnobiletin, DMF1, lysionotin, gardenin B, 3-OH-flavone, cirsimaritin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: O-methylated hydroxyflavones and PMFs including nobiletin, sinensetin, SAL, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Up-regulated in PMF-rich Red tangerine flavedo; highly correlated with total PMFs and six monomers; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue type and developmental stage; transient overexpression/RNAi in flavedo
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal modifying enzyme; major determinant of PMF variation, but formal rate-limiting designation not established
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter analyzed comparatively; specific functional promoter dissection not reported for CreOMT5
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RNA-seq (PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912); BSA/pool sequencing (PRJNA938971)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful target for metabolic engineering and PMF biofortification in citrus
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Could not O-methylate genkwanin in vitro. Exhibits neofunctionalized activity relative to OMT6, including C3 methylation capability in the OMT4/5 lineage context discussed by authors.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Clementine and other citrus accessions for homolog comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata Clementine genome assembly/scaffold-based reference; exact version NA
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Related to polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis; proposed ancestral enzyme of OMT cluster
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream terminal modification-related enzyme, but functionally weaker than OMT cluster enzymes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies limited/weak direct methylation capacity related to target product; ancestral paralog to major PMF enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not associated with PMF content variation in the mapping/correlation analysis; shows weak or deficient PMF-related O-methylation relative to OMT cluster enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT6 is highly homologous to CreOMT3/4/5 and lies ~1.6 Mb downstream of the OMT cluster. 2. It was not associated with PMF content variation despite expression correlation with PMF content during Red tangerine fruit development. 3. Recombinant OMT6 homologs from most citrus species weakly methylated or failed to methylate hydroxyflavones at C3, C5, and C7. 4. Functional divergence from CreOMT4 is partly explained by amino acid residue D17 versus N17. 5. The OMT cluster likely arose from duplication and neofunctionalization of OMT6.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics and synteny; cloning of OMT6 homologs; recombinant protein assays; expression-PMF correlation; site-directed mutagenesis comparing CreOMT6 and CreOMT4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes weak or limited O-methylation of hydroxyflavones; wild-type CreOMT6 lacks the broad multisite activity of the neofunctionalized OMT cluster enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones used in OMT assays such as GAD and 3HF and others in substrate panel
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Limited O-methylated hydroxyflavone products; specific products for CreOMT6 not comprehensively detailed in main text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Negligible in Group I and II citrus lacking the OMT cluster; in Red tangerine expression correlates with PMF content during development but not with inter-accession PMF variation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; species group context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; not the major control locus for PMF natural variation
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter compared among citrus groups; no detailed functional assay except general promoter sequence comparison
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912); comparative genomic datasets; BSA context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Mainly evolutionary insight; weaker immediate breeding value than OMT cluster genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: D17N substitution in CreOMT6 confers gained C3/C5/C6/C3' O-methylation activity, indicating that residue 17 is crucial for functional divergence.

Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: A. buxfoliata genome assembly used in comparative analysis; exact version NA
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Related/ancestral branch of citrus PMF biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream O-methylation enzyme related to ancestral PMF pathway capacity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: PMFs were not detectable in A. buxfoliata fruit flavedo in this study
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Can directly methylate hydroxyflavone intermediates in vitro but does not contribute in vivo in its native species because expression is negligible
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Biochemically active OMT6 homolog with robust multisite O-methylation in vitro, yet native species lacks detectable PMFs due to lack of expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbOMT6 from A. buxfoliata is likely an ancient gene related to the OMT cluster and other citrus OMT6 genes. 2. Recombinant AbOMT6 methylated eight substrates at C5, C6, C7, C3' and C4' positions and showed robust activity similar to CreOMT4 and CreOMT5. 3. Despite this catalytic competence, AbOMT6 is not expressed in primitive citrus A. buxfoliata, explaining the absence of PMFs in vivo. 4. AbOMT6 lacks the C3 O-methylation activity that characterizes neofunctionalized CreOMT4 and CreOMT5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; cloning from A. buxfoliata; recombinant protein production; in vitro substrate panel enzyme assays; expression comparison across species
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers methyl groups from SAM to multiple hydroxyl positions of hydroxyflavones, including C5, C6, C7, C3' and C4', but not reported to catalyze C3 O-methylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Eight tested hydroxyflavone substrates from the assay panel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Not expressed or negligibly expressed in A. buxfoliata in the tissues analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Species context; recombinant in vitro assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter present; no promoter activity assay reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics; expression comparison; recombinant biochemical data
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful as an evolutionary and biochemical template for engineering multisite O-methylation, but limited direct breeding utility in citrus without expression activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Provides evidence that catalytic capacity alone is insufficient for PMF accumulation; expression evolution and later neofunctionalization were both important.

Title: Neofunctionalization of an OMT cluster dominates polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis associated with the domestication of citrus
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
